intelligenceGaps.title: Narrative Fragmentation
intelligenceGaps.description: Expansion into Xiaohongshu and Threads shows significant tone variance compared to LinkedIn. Consistency scores dropped by 0.4 points this month.
intelligenceGaps.recommendation: Align brand voice guidelines across all emerging platforms.

intelligenceGaps.title: Engagement Leakage
intelligenceGaps.description: High engagement on TikTok and Facebook is not translating to website visits. CTAs across emerging channels remain unoptimized.
intelligenceGaps.recommendation: Implement trackable CTAs and cross-channel journey mapping.

intelligenceGaps.title: Trust but Weak Conversion
intelligenceGaps.description: High credibility scores from testimonials are not being leveraged at the point of action. Missing CTAs on landing pages.
intelligenceGaps.recommendation: Embed social proof directly into conversion funnels.

narrativeSummary: Acme Corp maintains a complex multi-channel profile. While Google Maps and LinkedIn anchor the brand's professional credibility, new visual-first channels like Xiaohongshu and TikTok are driving high engagement but creating narrative friction. The expansion into Threads shows a brand attempting real-time relevance, yet the core conversion engine (Website) remains disconnected from this new energy, leading to a visible 'engagement-to-action' gap across the digital ecosystem.

competitor.name: Competitor X
advantages: Higher Local Trust score in North America (+15%)
Active engagement on B2B visual channels (Instagram)
opportunities: Weaker Video Presence compared to Competitor X
Lower backlink credibility in trade publications
Consistency gaps in customer support response times
competitor.executiveSummary: Acme Corp maintains a strong lead in Digital Clarity and Consistency. The messaging architecture is significantly more cohesive across the web and Instagram. However, Competitor X is leveraging a robust YouTube strategy that has granted them higher authority in technical credibility segments. To bridge the gap, Acme Corp should prioritize long-form video content and industry whitepapers.
